commands.review_summary:
The PR routes Google Veo REST fallback operation JSON reads through `readResponseWithLimit` with a 16 MiB cap and adds an oversized streamed-response regression test.

PR surface: Source +9, Tests +60. Total +69 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source, though I did not run a local repro in this read-only review. Current main uses an unbounded `response.text()` in the shared Google video REST operation helper, and the PR fixture exercises the exported provider path with an oversized stream.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] No automated repair is queued because the code review found no blocking patch defect; the remaining blockers are contributor proof and maintainer acceptance of the cap.

target.title: fix(google): bound Veo video operation response reads
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

This bounds **only** the Google **Veo video** REST fallback operation read in `extensions/google/video-generation-provider.ts` (`requestGoogleVideoJson`), which used an unbounded `await response.text()` before checking `response.ok`. The Veo create/poll operation response is external provider content; a hostile or misconfigured endpoint can stream an arbitrarily large body that is fully buffered into memory — a memory-pressure / hang (OOM) vector. (Scope is the video-generation operation JSON only — distinct from #96324, which covers Google embedding/image/media/oauth/speech and does not touch `video-generation-provider.ts`.)

## Evidence

Reuses the shared bounded reader `readResponseWithLimit` (16 MiB cap, cancels the stream on overflow) — same pattern as the recently merged #95218 / #96027 / #96038. Plugin-scoped; the `response.ok` / HTTP-detail / success `JSON.parse` flow is otherwise unchanged. create and poll share `requestGoogleVideoJson`, so both paths are covered.

Real behavior proof (live vitest, not mocked): a real `ReadableStream` streaming a 64 MiB body is fed to the exported request path, asserting `Google video operation response exceeds 16777216 bytes` is thrown, `wasCanceled() === true`, `getReadCount() < 64`.

```
Tests  16 passed (16)
```
